sanskrit:
शुभाशुभफलैरेवं मोक्ष्यसे कर्मबन्धनैः।
संन्यासयोगयुक्तात्मा विमुक्तो मामुपैष्यसि॥
transliteration:
śubhāśubha-phalair evaṃ mokṣyase karma-bandhanaiḥ |
sannyāsa-yoga-yuktātmā vimukto mām upaiṣyasi ||
literal_meaning: Thus by (good-and-evil) results indeed you will be freed from action-bonds; having the self yoked to the yoga of renunciation, liberated, you will attain Me.
contextual_meaning: When every action is offered to the Whole, the mind is released from the ledger of merit and guilt; identity shifts from doer to instrument, dissolving existential debt and opening a direct path to felt union with the transcendent.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic freedom: the nervous system down-regulates when the inner critic is no longer needed to keep score.
trigger_scenario: A burnt-out caregiver learns to dedicate each small act of service to something bigger than personal reward; the inner ledger of ‘good/bad daughter’ quiets and she sleeps through the night for the first time in years.
anchor_text: Offer every deed to the Whole and walk free of merit and guilt.
